provenance.title: DNAGPT: A Generalized Pre-trained Tool for Versatile DNA Sequence Analysis Tasks
affiliations: Southern University of Science and Technology
provenance.abstract: DNAGPT is a generalized GPT-based DNA pre-training model trained on over 200 billion base pairs from all mammals. It augments the classic GPT decoder with (1) a binary classification pre-training task (DNA sequence order), (2) a numerical regression pre-training task (guanine-cytosine content prediction), and (3) a comprehensive token language that encodes sequence, number, and task/instruction information in one token space. Dedicated sequential + numerical embedding layers and classification + regression decoding heads let a single model process both DNA sequence and numerical data. It outperforms task-specific models on genomic signal & region (GSR) recognition, mRNA abundance regression, and artificial human genome generation.
key_claims: A single GPT-based model jointly handles DNA sequence AND numerical data via dual embeddings (Sequential/Numerical) and dual heads (Classification/Regression)
Two DNA-specific pre-training tasks beyond next-token prediction: GC-content regression (MSE) and sequence-order binary classification (CE)
A hierarchical token language (sequence/number/instruction/connection/reserved tokens) unifies diverse task formats (classification, regression, generation) in one token space
Non-overlapping k-mer tokenization gives N/k tokens (k-fold efficiency vs overlapped k-mers)

DNAGPT beats DeepGSR/GSRNET on GSR recognition, Xpresso on mRNA abundance (r2 0.59->0.62 human), and GAN/RBM on artificial human genome generation; more pre-training data (human -> 9 species -> all mammals) improves downstream performance

architecture.primary_variant: DNAGPT-M (0.1B): 12-layer GPT decoder, hidden 768, 12 heads, unidirectional attention, seq len 4096 (24,576 bp), 9-species pretrain

modules.name: NonOverlapKmerTokenizer
modules.notes: Non-overlapped k-mers: shift == k, yields N/k tokens.

modules.name: SequentialEmbedding
modules.notes: Embeds DNA-sequence + special tokens (Kmers separation then embedding layers).

modules.name: NumericalEmbedding
modules.notes: Numbers sent directly into an MLP-based numerical embedding, co-trained with DNA embeddings; concatenated with sequence embeddings before the GPT.

modules.name: GPTDecoderLayer
modules.notes: Classic GPT block x12 (0.1B). 3B variant: 60 layers, hidden 2048, 64 heads.

training_pipeline.curriculum: 3 joint pretraining tasks (NTP + GC regression + sequence order). DNAGPT-H 15 epochs on ~3B bp (8xV100, ~1 day); DNAGPT-S-512 2 epochs on ~200B bp (~1 week).

datasets.name: DeepGSR GSR datasets (PAS + TIS: human/mouse/bovine/fruit fly)

evaluation_protocol.compute: Pretrain: 0.1B on 8xV100 32GB (~1 day for H); S-512 ~1 week; 3B on 16xV100. Deepspeed + FSDP. Finetune fits on a single GPU.
special_conditions: GSR motif removed in preprocessing (model must use flanking non-coding context)
300 bp before+after GSR concatenated (seq len 603 TIS / 606 PAS)
negative samples from chromosome with GC nearest to genome average
finetune 10 epochs, lr = 1/3 of pretrain lr
mRNA task: joint sequence+number input, regression head, r2 metric

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Load official DNAGPT weights (dna_gpt0.1b_m.pth) from the authors' TencentAILabHealthcare/DNAGPT repo (Google Drive / Weiyun) rather than pretraining
implementation_assumptions.basis: pretraining on 200B bp across many V100s is infeasible; official .pth checkpoints + code are public

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Weights are custom .pth (not on HF Hub); loading uses the authors' custom model class, not AutoModel
implementation_assumptions.basis: repo ships test.py + custom modeling; checkpoints hosted on Google Drive/Weiyun
alternatives: re-implement architecture from scratch and load state_dict by key mapping

implementation_assumptions.assumption: GSR recognition = binary classification via classification head on the sequence; ACC/F1/MCC reported
Non-overlap 6-mer tokenization with A/C/G/T/N vocab (19,530 sequence tokens)

ambiguities.location: Numerical embedding / regression head exact MLP shapes
ambiguities.description: Paper says 'MLPs' for numerical embed + regression head but does not give layer sizes.
Fig S3 lists betas (0.9, 0.98) with momentum 0.937 (unusual to have both). Standard AdamW has no momentum field.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Use AdamW betas (0.9, 0.98); ignore 'momentum' (likely a config artifact)

ambiguities.description: 0.1B models use 3e-5, 3B uses 3e-6, S-512 uses 1e-5 on NT (Fig S4).
